text: The wcc386 compiler shall support a new -elf command line switch to specify ELF object file format.
The wcc386 compiler shall support a new -zpic command line switch to enable Position-Independent Code generation.
The CG386 code generator shall exclude the EBX register from normal register allocation when PIC generation is enabled.
The compiler shall generate a Position-Independent function prologue that computes the absolute address of the Global Offset Table into the EBX register.
The compiler shall save the EBX, ESI, and EDI registers on the stack during the function prologue for both PIC and PDC.
The compiler shall restore all saved registers from the stack during the function epilogue.
The compiler shall generate R_386_PLT32 relocation types for direct function calls when PIC is enabled.
The compiler shall reference global data through offsets in the Global Offset Table using the EBX register as a base.
The compiler shall use R_386_GOTOFF relocations for position-independent references to static data.
The CG386 code generator shall implement ELF object file output by mapping OMF entities to OWL functions.
The OWL library shall be extended to support 386-specific relocation types including R_386_GOT32, R_386_GOTOFF, R_386_GOTPC, and R_386_PLT32.
The wlink linker shall support building ELF shared objects using the 'form ELF DLL' command line option.
The linker shall set the ELF header e_type field to ET_DYN when building shared objects.
The linker shall include a PT_DYNAMIC program header in shared objects to specify dynamic linking information.
The linker shall create a .dynamic section containing the dynamic array tags required by the System V ABI.
The linker shall generate both a normal symbol table (.symtab) and a dynamic symbol table (.dynsym) for shared objects.
The linker shall merge all dynamic relocations into a single relocation table named .rel.dyn for shared objects.
The linker shall build a Global Offset Table (GOT) when R_386_GOT32, R_386_GOTOFF, or R_386_GOTPC relocations are encountered.
The linker shall reserve the first three entries of the Global Offset Table as specified by the 386 ABI.
The linker shall define the symbol *GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE* at the base of the .got section.
The linker shall create a Procedure Linkage Table (PLT) when R_386_PLT32 relocations are encountered for STT_FUNC symbols.
The linker shall create R_386_JUMP_SLOT relocations for PLT entries to support lazy binding.
The linker shall support reading existing ELF shared objects using the Object Reading Library (ORL).
The linker shall include a PT_INTERP program header in executables that use shared objects, specifying /lib/ld-linux.so.2 as the path.
The linker shall record shared object dependencies using DT_NEEDED entries in the dynamic section.
The linker must disable 4-byte offset correction for R_386_PC32 relocations when an implicit addend is specified in the ELF file.
The ORL shall treat STT_NOTYPE symbols as normal symbols to prevent fatal linker errors during dietlibc linking.
The linker shall adjust the p_memsz field in the program header to account for .bss section allocation and page alignment.
